task_completion.description: Modify the Jitsi Meet web configuration file to require a display name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (e.g., nano, vim, gedit) or terminal output showing the file '/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js' being edited to include the setting 'requireDisplayName: true'.

task_completion.description: Navigate to a Jitsi Meet meeting URL to trigger the pre-join screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser open to a Jitsi Meet URL, displaying the pre-join screen where users configure their audio/video and enter their name before joining.

task_completion.description: Verify the join button is disabled when the name field is empty.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Jitsi Meet pre-join screen in the browser. The display name input field must be empty, and the 'Join meeting' button must be visibly disabled (e.g., greyed out or unclickable).

task_completion.description: Save the screenshot of the disabled button to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot utility being used, a terminal command saving an image, or a file manager showing the file 'evidence_disabled_button.png' located in the '/home/ga/' directory.

Look for the main Jitsi Meet active meeting interface in the browser. The user should have entered a name and clicked join, transitioning from the pre-join screen to the actual meeting room (showing the meeting toolbar, self-view, or participant list).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with a real web browser to test the policy.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that a standard web browser (like Chromium or Firefox) is used to render the Jitsi Meet interface, rather than terminal curl commands or text-based browsers.

integrity.description: Ensure the screenshot evidence was genuinely captured from the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check for the use of a screenshot tool or command (e.g., gnome-screenshot, scrot, or browser dev tools) capturing the actual screen state, rather than copying a pre-existing image.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually joined a functional Jitsi meeting.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the final meeting interface displays standard Jitsi UI elements (like the bottom toolbar, hangup button, and participant thumbnails) indicating a live application state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct configuration file to modify is '/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js'. To enforce the policy, the setting 'requireDisplayName' must be set to 'true'. The evidence screenshot must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/evidence_disabled_button.png'.
